H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong. Largest sample in HEALING enables strong confidence, with 66% positive responses. Highest YouTube engagement (30 views) provides initial retention signal despite null avg_view_duration. Qualitative (n=29) shows rapid settle patterns (11 at 'About 10 minutes', 7 at 'About 5 minutes') and strong positive music ratings (10 'Liked it', 9 'Loved it!'). AutoGluon predicted 6.859 — observed 7.15 exceeds model estimate, suggesting possible audience selection effects or model underestimation.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Nearly 100 people have tried this overstimulation protocol — the majority feel much calmer afterward, with many saying the music and rhythm feel just right.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong. Second-largest overstimulation protocol shows robust outcome with similar dispersion to L1. Qualitative feedback (n=21) indicates 10/21 'Noticeable improvement' with 'Very settling' rhythm ratings (7) — consistent with overstimulation targeting. YouTube reach very low (5 views), limiting engagement signal interpretation.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners generally report feeling better after this overstimulation protocol, though the music style gets mixed reactions — some find it very settling, others neutral.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful. Tightest distribution in overstimulation subset. Qualitative (n=23) shows 10/22 'Balanced / comfortable' rhythm ratings but 4/22 'Slightly busy', suggesting groove-acceptance tension. Highest 'Loved it!' music ratings (11) but lower 'Balanced' rhythm — some listeners may prefer different pacing. Notably reduced cross-run drift (0.784) compared to L1/L2.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Most listeners enjoy the music here, though some find the rhythm slightly active — overall, this protocol helps people calm down effectively.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong. The highest-performing DESCENT protocol in HEALING shows strong positive outcome clustering (63% positive), though outcome distribution reveals notable dispersion across the full 0-10 range. Qualitative data (n=23) shows most listeners settled within 10 minutes with 'Balanced / comfortable' rhythm ratings (13/23), supporting the protocol's design for anxiety regulation. YouTube reach remains low (7 views) precluding retention analysis. AutoGluon predicted 7.838 for the anxiety zone — observed 7.22 sits marginally below, suggesting either mild overestimation in the model or audience-specific variance effects.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Most listeners find this anxiety protocol helpful — nearly two-thirds report feeling noticeably better after listening, with many settling into a calmer state within about ten minutes.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful. Tightest distribution in the anxiety subset indicates more consistent listener response patterns to this groove variant. Qualitative feedback shows split between 'Liked it' (9) and 'Neutral / so-so' (5) on music — this groove sensitivity may be modulating outcomes. AutoGluon predicted 7.838 for anxiety zone — observed 6.94 suggests potential subpopulation mismatch or groove acceptance variation.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: This anxiety protocol works well for many, though some listeners find the music style just okay — like trying a different flavor and seeing how it lands.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful. Distribution shows right-skewed tail with 5/36 respondents scoring 10, indicating strong ceiling responders. However, cross-run drift at 0.695 indicates evolving interpretation across analysis cycles. Qualitative notes (n=20) show 8/20 report 'Noticeable improvement' but 2/20 report 'No effect', suggesting subgroup variation not captured by aggregate mean. No YouTube retention data available yet.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Listeners generally find this anxiety protocol helpful, though a small number don't notice much change — the effects seem to vary depending on the person.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.7, confidence=meaningful. As expected for a crisis DESCENT protocol, the floor-biased distribution reflects listeners arriving in acute distress states. Notably, 12/43 listeners scored 9-10 despite the low baseline, supporting the protocol's deep-descent mechanism effectiveness. AutoGluon predicted 7.955 — significantly above observed 6.35, suggesting potential under-reporting by crisis-state listeners or model overestimation for this acute population.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Crisis protocols naturally show lower scores because people start in acute distress — but over half report meaningful improvement after listening.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.7, confidence=meaningful. Distribution mirrors L1 with similar variance structure. Qualitative data (n=16) shows mixed music opinions: 5 'Loved it!' but 6 'Neutral / so-so', suggesting groove acceptance variability in the crisis population. AutoGluon predicted 7.955 — observed 6.52 indicates potential predictive gap for crisis protocols.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: This crisis protocol shows moderate improvement overall, with mixed reactions to the music style — some love it, others are neutral.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful. Highest variance in the crisis subset, with 5/27 scoring 9-10, consistent with protocol purpose. Qualitative data shows 7 listeners settled 'Immediately / within first minute' vs 5 at 'About 5 minutes' — acoustic response divergence warrants investigation. Cross-run drift at 0.711 indicates analytical evolution.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Many people settle quickly with this crisis protocol, though some find the rhythm a bit active — most still notice improvement.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong. Strong efficacy for Sleep & Rest despite clinical population mix (6/54 clients/patients). Qualitative (n=15) shows 5/15 settled 'Immediately / within first minute' — unusually fast entrainment for sleep protocol. High self-listener ratio (13/15) may skew positive. AutoGluon predicted 7.539 — observed 7.19 shows close alignment, supporting stable efficacy.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: This sleep protocol works well, with many people falling asleep quickly — the music feels balanced and comfortable for most listeners.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful. Most constrained variance in sleep set. Qualitative (n=15) shows 3/15 'No effect' responses amid otherwise high ratings — possible subgroup that requires different groove or timing (note: 5/15 were clients/patients vs 4/15 self-listeners). YouTube reach minimal (5 views).
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: This sleep protocol is popular with caregivers, though some listeners don't notice much effect — the music style is received more as okay than loved.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful. Bimodal hint visible in distribution with clusters at 6 and 10. Qualitative (n=22) shows 4/22 rhythm rated as 'Slightly busy' — likely influencing low-end outcome tails. Cross-run drift at 0.785, lower than sibling sleep protocols, indicates analytical stability.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: The rhythm feels balanced for most, though a few find it slightly active — overall this sleep protocol helps people settle into rest.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful. Lowest within THRIVING focus cluster with notable variance. Qualitative reveals 9/23 rhythm rated as 'Slightly busy' — this may be contributing to the lower mean and higher dispersion. 10/23 reported 'Noticeable improvement', but high variance suggests inconsistent fit. Panel divided on whether routing or design drives this anomaly.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: This focus protocol gets mixed results — some feel clearer, but the slightly busy rhythm may not work for everyone, with noticeable variance in outcomes.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.6, confidence=meaningful. Strong positive signal close to sibling L3 despite lower ceiling. Mixed music opinions (6 'Neutral/so-so', 5 'Loved it!') with balanced rhythm (8/17). YouTube reach minimal (4 views). AutoGluon predicted 7.728 for focus zone — observed 7.19 aligns closely.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: This focus protocol generally works well for most listeners, though a small portion don't feel much effect — the rhythm tends toward comfortable.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful. Highest mean in THRIVING, with density in top bins (10 scores 9-10). Qualitative strong: 11/29 'Significant improvement', 12/29 'Liked it' music. However, 3/29 'No effect' reports represent potential subgroup mismatch. AutoGluon predicted high (7.728) — observed slightly below despite strong signal. YouTube views (15) developing.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: This focus protocol is our strongest performer — most listeners report significant improvement, with balanced rhythm and high music appreciation.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful. Lower than THRIVING average for creative flow HOLD protocol. Qualitative notes delayed onset (6 settled 'Immediately', 6 at 'About 10 minutes') — consistent with hold-phase nature. AutoGluon predicted 7.728 — observed 6.74 significantly below suggests mismatch, possibly requiring adjusted hold phase duration or routing refinements.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Many listeners feel slightly to noticeably improved with this creative flow protocol, though settling times vary widely from immediate to ten minutes.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful. Tightest distribution in creative flow set despite smaller n. Highest 'Loved it!' music ratings (6) in creative subset supports hold-protocol efficacy. No clear cross-bucket pattern in AutoGluon output for this variant.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners are split on this creative flow protocol — some love it, others find it moderate, with most settling after about ten minutes.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing. Highest mean in THRIVING with tightest distribution — strong efficacy signal in early data. Qualitative (n=25) shows balanced rhythm (14/25 'Balanced / comfortable') and high positive change ratings. AutoGluon predicted 7.728 — observed 7.28 slightly below estimate despite small n, suggesting reliable efficacy if sample grows.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Most listeners feel noticeably better with this creative flow protocol, and the rhythm feels comfortable — a strong performer despite smaller sample size.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful. Tightest distribution in maintenance set indicates uniform response. Qualitative shows mixed music opinions (6 'Neutral / so-so', 6 'Liked it') yet high efficacy — music-rhythm decoupling may indicate protocol works through different mechanisms. 9/23 settled 'About 20 minutes', longest in series.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: This maintenance protocol delivers consistent results for most, though people often need about 20 minutes to fully settle into its effects.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful. Widest variance in maintenance subset. Qualitative: 3/16 'Slight improvement', indicating potential audience mismatch. AutoGluon predicted 7.693 — observed significantly below (6.68), suggesting this variant may need routing refinement or groove adjustment.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: This maintenance protocol shows weaker results with more variance than siblings — some notice improvement, but the fit may not work for everyone.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing. Highest satisfaction in maintenance series despite small n. Qualitative concern: 4/19 'Did NOT settle' responses indicate retention issues that may affect aggregate scoring. High variance (std=1.73) with bimodal hint suggests subgroup differentiation.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: This maintenance protocol is developing a positive reputation despite some retention challenges — some listeners don't settle at all.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful. High 'Loved it!' ratings (10) suggest music succeeds, but moderate mean indicates rhythm may not match all listener needs. 6/22 adviser attempts. Highest YouTube retention in TRANSFORMING with 8 views. Predicted 8.230 for zone — observed 6.74 significantly below suggests model overestimation.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: This energy protocol shows moderate improvement overall, with good music appreciation but rhythm that doesn't match every listener's preference.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=25, mean=6.76, std=2.05, confidence=meaningful. HIGHEST std in entire HEALING and THRIVING cohorts; second-highest in library overall. Bimodal distribution strongly indicated despite small n. Qualitative shows 7/15 'Significant improvement' but 0 'None' in change ratings — suggests polarised response pattern. Cross-run drift 0.532 is concerning.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: This energy protocol has the widest variance in responses — significant improvement for some, but likely a real subgroup that doesn't respond well.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing. Lowest YouTube views in series (4) limits engagement signals. Bimodal hint with cluster at 4 and 7. Qualitative 9/25 'Very settling' rhythm — some listeners clearly benefit while others don't. Cross-run drift 0.627 indicates analytical evolution.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: This energy protocol shows moderate effects with most finding it settling, though four views is too few to draw strong conclusions yet.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful. Low floor consistent with ascending-arc design — listeners in low-affect states may report moderate baseline. 2/17 'No effect' responses. Highest drop-off rate in first third — initial engagement may need strengthening. Predicted 7.876 — observed significantly below.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: This motivation protocol has lower average scores and some drop-off early on — the pacing might not grab everyone initially.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing. Lowest within motivation subset. Notable cross-run drift (0.585) indicates analytical evolution. 7/21 qualitative responses report no effect — possible groove mismatch or suboptimal routing. Predicted 7.876, observed far below.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: This motivation protocol shows mixed results — many feel improved but several report no effect at all, with only seven views so far.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing. Highest mean in motivation set — surprising given small n and high variance. Bimodal distribution with strong tails at both 2 and 9 — suggests this variant serves some listeners extremely well while poorly serving others. Cross-run drift at 0.507 (LOWEST in entire library). Development priority for L2 variant.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: This motivation protocol has very mixed results — very high scores for some but significantly lower for others, indicating strong subgroup effects.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful. High music ratings but 2/25 'It actually made things worse' — concerning for a clarity protocol meant to enhance awareness. Cross-run drift notable (0.655). Predicted 7.538 — observed below suggests efficacy ceiling or adverse subgroup needs identification.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Most like the music here, but two listeners reported things got worse — this clarity protocol may not work for everyone and needs investigation.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing. Slightly tighter distribution than L1 in mindclarity subset. Highest YouTube views (12) in TRANSFORMING. Rhythm ratings 12/20 'Very settling' — suggests this variant successfully reaches intended low-arousal population. Predicted 7.538 vs observed below.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: This clarity protocol shows good engagement with notably settling rhythms — twelve people have viewed it, indicating growing interest.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45, confidence=developing. LOWEST mean in the library with HIGHEST variance — signals critical design or routing issue. Distribution shows bifurcation (peaks at 3 and 9), consistent with two distinct listener populations not being served. Qualitative shows 9/17 'Noticeable improvement' but 4/17 'No effect' — confirms bimodal split. ALL FIVE ANALYZERS flagged this as priority anomaly.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: This clarity protocol is our lowest performer — responses split sharply between those who feel much clearer and those who feel nothing, suggesting a fundamental mismatch.

sphere_commentary.overview:
Across 1000 outcome responses, the three series show differentiated efficacy profiles aligning with design intent: HEALING maintains the strongest consistency (mean=6.98), THRIVING achieves the highest means in cognitive focus protocols (mean=6.96), and TRANSFORMING exhibits the expected widest variance (mean=6.59), suggesting its ascending-arc target is reaching appropriately diverse listeners.
• HEALING protocols demonstrate robust baseline efficacy with 12 protocols achieving mean scores between 6.35-7.22.
• THRIVING shows strong focus/clarity outcomes, particularly T-FC-L3 (mean=7.63) and T-CF-L3 (mean=7.28).
• TRANSFORMING protocols warrant particular attention: X-MC-L3 (mean=5.7, std=2.45) represents the lowest performer in the entire library.
• YouTube retention data remains sparse (NULL for most protocols with <50 views), limiting engagement signal interpretation.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
The three series maintain distinct response profiles consistent with their architectural intent.
• HEALING (n=574, mean=6.98, std=1.77): DESCENT protocols effectively regulate nervous system arousal, with lowest variance across the library.
• THRIVING (n=245, mean=6.96, std=1.72): HOLD protocols demonstrate strong cognitive/emotional optimization, with T-FC-L3 showing the highest single-protocol mean.
• TRANSFORMING (n=181, mean=6.59, std=1.87): ASCENT/DESCENT protocols exhibit expected wider spread given their low-affect state targeting, though X-MC-L3 (mean=5.7) may indicate a design or routing mismatch.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols show response patterns that diverge from their design intent and warrant investigation.
• X-MC-L3 (TRANSFORMING, n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45): The lowest mean and highest variance in the library suggests either a fundamental design issue or severe audience mismatch — concurrent bimodal distribution (peaks at scores 3 and 9) indicates two distinct listener subgroups not being served.
• T-FC-L1 (THRIVING, n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82): Notably lower than sibling protocols in the focus cluster, with qualitative data showing misalignment between outcome scores and reported rhythm comfort — 9/23 listeners rated rhythm as 'Slightly busy'.
• X-ME-L2 (TRANSFORMING, n=25, std=2.05): Highest standard deviation in the library, indicating potential population heterogeneity that current routing may not address.
• X-MD-L3 (TRANSFORMING, n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38): Despite being a developing-confidence protocol, shows strong mean but extreme variance suggesting inconsistent suitability.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Actionable protocol development opportunities emerge from cross-analyzer convergence.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3: All five analyzers flag low mean and high variance — investigate L2 variant with modified frequency anchoring or create separate pathway for 'settling' vs 'energy' seekers.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MD-L3: Multiple analyzers note notable cross-run drift (sim=0.507) and high variance — create L2 variant with extended descent phase.
• T-FC-L1: Investigate whether 'Slightly busy' rhythm ratings correlate with the drop in mean, and adjust rhythmic pacing for this specific protocol.
• H-CR-L1: AutoGluon predicted 7.955 vs observed 6.35 — investigate whether crisis listeners are under-reporting due to floor effects or if routing is suboptimal.
• X-ME-L1: High YouTube reach (8 views) but moderate outcome — test whether introduction modifications could improve pre-entrainment retention.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding signals indicate significant analytical evolution across runs.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: Major drift detected across 27/30 protocols between earliest and most recent runs, with X-MD-L3 (sim=0.507), X-ME-L2 (sim=0.532), and X-MD-L2 (sim=0.585) showing the lowest cosine similarities — this suggests either genuine data pattern shifts or analytical instability requiring monitoring.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Insufficient protocol density for reliable clustering — too few protocols currently have embeddings across multiple runs.
• Confidence: The drift signals may reflect protocol refinement impacts rather than noise, and continued monitoring is essential as the embedding history expands.

Qualitative shows mixed music opinions (6 'Neutral / so-so', 6 'Liked it') yet high efficacy — music-rhythm decoupling may indicate protocol works through different mechanisms. 9/23 settled 'About 20 minutes', longest in series.
by_protocol.T-MB-L2: VOL001: n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful. Widest variance in maintenance subset. Qualitative: 3/16 'Slight improvement', indicating potential audience mismatch. AutoGluon predicted 7.693 — observed significantly below (6.68), suggesting this variant may need routing refinement or groove adjustment.
by_protocol.T-MB-L3: VOL001: n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing. Highest satisfaction in maintenance series despite small n. Qualitative concern: 4/19 'Did NOT settle' responses indicate retention issues that may affect aggregate scoring. High variance (std=1.73) with bimodal hint suggests subgroup differentiation.
by_protocol.X-MC-L1: VOL001: n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful. High music ratings but 2/25 'It actually made things worse' — concerning for a clarity protocol meant to enhance awareness. Cross-run drift notable (0.655). Predicted 7.538 — observed below suggests efficacy ceiling or adverse subgroup needs identification.
by_protocol.X-MC-L2: VOL001: n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing. Slightly tighter distribution than L1 in mindclarity subset. Highest YouTube views (12) in TRANSFORMING. Rhythm ratings 12/20 'Very settling' — suggests this variant successfully reaches intended low-arousal population. Predicted 7.538 vs observed below.
by_protocol.X-MC-L3: VOL001: n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45, confidence=developing. LOWEST mean in the library with HIGHEST variance — signals critical design or routing issue. Distribution shows bifurcation (peaks at 3 and 9), consistent with two distinct listener populations not being served. Qualitative shows 9/17 'Noticeable improvement' but 4/17 'No effect' — confirms bimodal split. ALL FIVE ANALYZERS flagged this as priority anomaly.
by_protocol.X-MD-L1: VOL001: n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful. Low floor consistent with ascending-arc design — listeners in low-affect states may report moderate baseline. 2/17 'No effect' responses. Highest drop-off rate in first third — initial engagement may need strengthening. Predicted 7.876 — observed significantly below.
by_protocol.X-MD-L2: VOL001: n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing. Lowest within motivation subset. Notable cross-run drift (0.585) indicates analytical evolution. 7/21 qualitative responses report no effect — possible groove mismatch or suboptimal routing. Predicted 7.876, observed far below.
by_protocol.X-MD-L3: VOL001: n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing. Highest mean in motivation set — surprising given small n and high variance. Bimodal distribution with strong tails at both 2 and 9 — suggests this variant serves some listeners extremely well while poorly serving others. Cross-run drift at 0.507 (LOWEST in entire library). Development priority for L2 variant.
by_protocol.X-ME-L1: VOL001: n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful. High 'Loved it!' ratings (10) suggest music succeeds, but moderate mean indicates rhythm may not match all listener needs. 6/22 adviser attempts. Highest YouTube retention in TRANSFORMING with 8 views. Predicted 8.230 for zone — observed 6.74 significantly below suggests model overestimation.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: VOL001: n=25, mean=6.76, std=2.05, confidence=meaningful. HIGHEST std in entire HEALING and THRIVING cohorts; second-highest in library overall. Bimodal distribution strongly indicated despite small n. Qualitative shows 7/15 'Significant improvement' but 0 'None' in change ratings — suggests polarised response pattern. Cross-run drift 0.532 is concerning.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: VOL001: n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing. Lowest YouTube views in series (4) limits engagement signals. Bimodal hint with cluster at 4 and 7. Qualitative 9/25 'Very settling' rhythm — some listeners clearly benefit while others don't. Cross-run drift 0.627 indicates analytical evolution.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Most listeners find this anxiety protocol helpful — nearly two-thirds report feeling noticeably better after listening, with many settling into a calmer state within about ten minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: This anxiety protocol works well for many, though some listeners find the music style just okay — like trying a different flavor and seeing how it lands.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Listeners generally find this anxiety protocol helpful, though a small number don't notice much change — the effects seem to vary depending on the person.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Crisis protocols naturally show lower scores because people start in acute distress — but over half report meaningful improvement after listening.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: This crisis protocol shows moderate improvement overall, with mixed reactions to the music style — some love it, others are neutral.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Many people settle quickly with this crisis protocol, though some find the rhythm a bit active — most still notice improvement.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Nearly 100 people have tried this overstimulation protocol — the majority feel much calmer afterward, with many saying the music and rhythm feel just right.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Listeners generally report feeling better after this overstimulation protocol, though the music style gets mixed reactions — some find it very settling, others neutral.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Most listeners enjoy the music here, though some find the rhythm slightly active — overall, this protocol helps people calm down effectively.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: This sleep protocol works well, with many people falling asleep quickly — the music feels balanced and comfortable for most listeners.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: This sleep protocol is popular with caregivers, though some listeners don't notice much effect — the music style is received more as okay than loved.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: The rhythm feels balanced for most, though a few find it slightly active — overall this sleep protocol helps people settle into rest.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Many listeners feel slightly to noticeably improved with this creative flow protocol, though settling times vary widely from immediate to ten minutes.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Listeners are split on this creative flow protocol — some love it, others find it moderate, with most settling after about ten minutes.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Most listeners feel noticeably better with this creative flow protocol, and the rhythm feels comfortable — a strong performer despite smaller sample size.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: This focus protocol gets mixed results — some feel clearer, but the slightly busy rhythm may not work for everyone, with noticeable variance in outcomes.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: This focus protocol generally works well for most listeners, though a small portion don't feel much effect — the rhythm tends toward comfortable.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: This focus protocol is our strongest performer — most listeners report significant improvement, with balanced rhythm and high music appreciation.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: This maintenance protocol delivers consistent results for most, though people often need about 20 minutes to fully settle into its effects.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: This maintenance protocol shows weaker results with more variance than siblings — some notice improvement, but the fit may not work for everyone.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: This maintenance protocol is developing a positive reputation despite some retention challenges — some listeners don't settle at all.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Most like the music here, but two listeners reported things got worse — this clarity protocol may not work for everyone and needs investigation.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: This clarity protocol shows good engagement with notably settling rhythms — twelve people have viewed it, indicating growing interest.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: This clarity protocol is our lowest performer — responses split sharply between those who feel much clearer and those who feel nothing, suggesting a fundamental mismatch.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: This motivation protocol has lower average scores and some drop-off early on — the pacing might not grab everyone initially.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: This motivation protocol shows mixed results — many feel improved but several report no effect at all, with only seven views so far.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: This motivation protocol has very mixed results — very high scores for some but significantly lower for others, indicating strong subgroup effects.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: This energy protocol shows moderate improvement overall, with good music appreciation but rhythm that doesn't match every listener's preference.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: This energy protocol has the widest variance in responses — significant improvement for some, but likely a real subgroup that doesn't respond well.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: This energy protocol shows moderate effects with most finding it settling, though four views is too few to draw strong conclusions yet.
